title: CODE OF PRACTICE CONCERNING SOURCE DIRECTED MEASURES TO REDUCE CONTAMINATION OF FOOD WITH CHEMICALS

sections.section: CODE OF PRACTICE CONCERNING SOURCE DIRECTED MEASURES TO REDUCE CONTAMINATION OF FOOD WITH CHEMICALS
sections.summary: Non-substantive heading/title identifying the Code of Practice on source-directed measures to reduce chemical contamination of food.

Defines the scope and objective of CAC/RCP 49-2001 to address major environmental chemical sources contaminating food (excluding pesticides, veterinary drugs, additives, mycotoxins and natural toxins), promotes awareness and source-directed preventive measures, urges food control authorities to notify and engage relevant agencies, advocates prevention over end-product control (including HACCP analysis), and recommends measures such as emission and waste controls, restrictions on persistent toxic substances, premarket testing, and substitution of harmful chemicals.
